docs.content:
# mxfood

## Data Description
This db contains data about a food delivery company. You have 3 types of data:
- standard ops / transactional data about orders, users, deliveries, products etc
- events data (for user flows)
- marketing data (ads, ad spends, etc)

content.description: Top Important metrics for mxfood company
Weekly average order value
content.query:
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at) as week_start,
  AVG(total) as avg_order_value
FROM mxfood.orders
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at)
ORDER BY week_start

content.description: Weekly order count and Revenue
content.query:
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at) as week,
  COUNT(*) as orders,
  SUM(total) as revenue
FROM mxfood.orders
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at)
ORDER BY week

content.description: Weekly revenue split by product category (e.g., Pizza, Burgers, etc.)
content.query:
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('week', o.created_at) as week_start,
  pc.category_name,
  SUM(oi.total_price) as revenue
FROM mxfood.orders o
JOIN mxfood.order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN mxfood.products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
JOIN mxfood.product_subcategories ps ON p.subcategory_id = ps.subcategory_id
JOIN mxfood.product_categories pc ON ps.category_id = pc.category_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', o.created_at), pc.category_name
ORDER BY week_start, category_name

documents.name: Orders by Day of Week and Hour (Last Month)

content.description: Pivot table showing average completed orders by day of week and hour of day for the last month
content.query:
WITH hourly_counts AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('day', created_at) as order_date,
    CASE DAYOFWEEK(created_at)
      WHEN 0 THEN 'Sunday'
      WHEN 1 THEN 'Monday'
      WHEN 2 THEN 'Tuesday'
      WHEN 3 THEN 'Wednesday'
      WHEN 4 THEN 'Thursday'
      WHEN 5 THEN 'Friday'
      WHEN 6 THEN 'Saturday'
    END as day_of_week,
    CASE 
      WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) >= 0 AND EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) < 6 THEN '0-6 (Early Morning)'
      WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) >= 6 AND EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) < 12 THEN '6-12 (Morning)'
      WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) >= 12 AND EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) < 18 THEN '12-18 (Afternoon)'
      WHEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at) >= 18 THEN '18-24 (Evening)'
    END as hour_range,
    COUNT(*) as order_count
  FROM mxfood.orders
  WHERE status = 'completed'
    AND created_at >= '2025-12-01'
 GROUP BY order_date, day_of_week, hour_range
)
SELECT 
  day_of_week,
  hour_range,
  ROUND(AVG(order_count), 1) as avg_orders
FROM hourly_counts
GROUP BY day_of_week, hour_range
ORDER BY 
  CASE day_of_week
    WHEN 'Monday' THEN 1
    WHEN 'Tuesday' THEN 2
    WHEN 'Wednesday' THEN 3
    WHEN 'Thursday' THEN 4
    WHEN 'Friday' THEN 5
    WHEN 'Saturday' THEN 6
    WHEN 'Sunday' THEN 7
  END,
  CASE hour_range
    WHEN '0-6 (Early Morning)' THEN 1
    WHEN '6-12 (Morning)' THEN 2
    WHEN '12-18 (Afternoon)' THEN 3
    WHEN '18-24 (Evening)' THEN 4
  END

content.description: Completed orders by month
content.query: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month, COUNT(*) as orders FROM mxfood.orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

content.description: Total revenue per month
content.query: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month, ROUND(SUM(total), 2) as revenue FROM mxfood.orders WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

content.description: Count of unique users who placed orders each month
content.query: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as month, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as active_users FROM mxfood.orders GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) ORDER BY month

content.description: Number of new user signups each week
content.query:
WITH weekly_new_users AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at) as week,
    COUNT(*) as new_users
  FROM mxfood.users
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at)
),
weekly_returning_users AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('week', o.created_at) as week,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.user_id) as returning_users
  FROM mxfood.orders o
  JOIN mxfood.users u ON o.user_id = u.user_id
  WHERE DATE_TRUNC('week', o.created_at) > DATE_TRUNC('week', u.created_at)
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', o.created_at)
)
SELECT 
  COALESCE(n.week, r.week) as week,
  COALESCE(n.new_users, 0) as new_users,
  COALESCE(r.returning_users, 0) as returning_users
FROM weekly_new_users n
FULL OUTER JOIN weekly_returning_users r ON n.week = r.week
ORDER BY week

content.description: Pie chart showing the breakdown of all users by whether they currently have an active subscription
content.query:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN us.subscription_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Subscriber'
    ELSE 'Non-Subscriber'
  END as user_type,
  COUNT(DISTINCT u.user_id) as user_count
FROM mxfood.users u
LEFT JOIN mxfood.user_subscriptions us ON u.user_id = us.user_id
  AND us.status = 'active'
  AND us.started_at <= '2025-12-01' 
  AND (us.ended_at IS NULL OR us.ended_at >= '2025-12-01')
GROUP BY user_type
ORDER BY user_count DESC

content.description: Pie chart showing revenue breakdown by platform (iOS, Android, Web) for December 2025
content.query:
SELECT 
  platform,
  ROUND(SUM(total), 2) as revenue
FROM mxfood.orders
WHERE status = 'completed'
  AND created_at >= '2025-12-01'
  AND created_at < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY platform
ORDER BY revenue DESC

documents.name: Top 5 Zones by Revenue - Last Month

content.description: Bar chart showing the top 5 zones by revenue for December 2025
content.query:
SELECT 
  z.zone_name,
  ROUND(SUM(o.total), 2) as revenue
FROM mxfood.orders o
JOIN mxfood.zones z ON o.zone_id = z.zone_id
WHERE o.status = 'completed'
  AND o.created_at >= '2025-12-01'
  AND o.created_at < '2026-01-01'
GROUP BY z.zone_name
ORDER BY revenue DESC
LIMIT 5

content.description: Conversion funnel from app open to payment success
Total sessions per day over the last 90 days
content.query:
SELECT 
  DATE(event_timestamp) as date,
  platform,
  COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) as total_sessions
FROM mxfood.events
WHERE event_timestamp >= '2025-12-01'
GROUP BY DATE(event_timestamp), platform
ORDER BY date, platform

content.description: Daily average number of events per session
content.query:
SELECT 
  DATE_TRUNC('month', event_timestamp) as month_start,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), 2) as events_per_session,
  ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT session_id) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT user_id), 2) as avg_sessions_per_user_per_week
FROM mxfood.events
WHERE event_timestamp >= '2025-01-01'
GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', event_timestamp)
ORDER BY month_start

content.description: Daily conversion rate from app open to payment success
content.query:
WITH weekly_funnel AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('week', event_timestamp) as week,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_name = 'app_open' THEN user_id END) as app_opens,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_name = 'payment_success' THEN user_id END) as conversions
  FROM mxfood.events
  WHERE event_timestamp >= '2025-12-01'
  GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('week', event_timestamp)
)
SELECT 
  week,
  ROUND(conversions * 100.0 / NULLIF(app_opens, 0), 2) as conversion_rate_pct
FROM weekly_funnel
WHERE app_opens > 0
ORDER BY week

content.name: configs
content.description: Company configuration files

content.description: System logs

docs.content:
# internals

All tables live in the document DB (PGLite / Postgres). Use `created_at` for timestamps.

## file_events
Tracks every file operation in MinusX.
- `event_type`: integer — 0=created, 1=read_direct, 2=read_as_reference, 3=updated, 4=deleted
- `file_id`: FK → `files.id`
- `user_id`: FK → `users.id`
- `created_at`: timestamp

Useful joins:
- `JOIN files fi ON fi.id = fe.file_id` → `fi.name`, `fi.path`, `fi.type`
- Mode from path: `SPLIT_PART(fi.path, '/', 2)` e.g. `org`, `tutorial`, `internals`
- `LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = fe.user_id` → `u.email`, `u.role`

## llm_call_events
Tracks every LLM API call made by the agent.
- `model`: e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `total_tokens`, `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`
- `cost` (USD float8 — cast to numeric for ROUND: `ROUND(SUM(cost)::numeric, 4)`)
- `duration_s` (seconds float8)
- `finish_reason`, `conversation_id`
- `created_at`: timestamp

## query_execution_events
Tracks every query run via /api/query.
- `query_hash`: hash of query + params + connection
- `file_id`: FK → `files.id` (the question file that ran the query, if any)
- `database_name`: connection name
- `duration_ms`, `row_count`, `col_count`
- `was_cache_hit`: boolean
- `error`: text (null on success)
- `created_at`: timestamp

content.description: File read events broken down by mode and file type
content.query:
SELECT SPLIT_PART(fi.path, '/', 2) AS mode, fi.type AS file_type, COUNT(*) AS events
FROM file_events fe JOIN files fi ON fi.id = fe.file_id
WHERE fe.event_type = 1
GROUP BY mode, file_type ORDER BY events DESC

content.description: Number of files created per day, broken down by mode
content.query:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', fe.created_at) AS day, SPLIT_PART(fi.path, '/', 2) AS mode, COUNT(*) AS files_created
FROM file_events fe JOIN files fi ON fi.id = fe.file_id
WHERE fe.event_type = 0
GROUP BY day, mode ORDER BY day

content.description: Top 20 most viewed files
content.query:
SELECT fi.name AS file_name, fi.path AS file_path, fi.type AS file_type, COUNT(*) AS views
FROM file_events fe JOIN files fi ON fi.id = fe.file_id
WHERE fe.event_type = 1
GROUP BY fi.name, fi.path, fi.type ORDER BY views DESC LIMIT 20

content.description: Top 20 users by total events
content.query:
SELECT u.email AS user_email, u.role AS user_role, COUNT(*) AS total_events,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN fe.event_type = 1 THEN 1 END) AS views,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN fe.event_type = 3 THEN 1 END) AS edits,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN fe.event_type = 0 THEN 1 END) AS creates
FROM file_events fe LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = fe.user_id
WHERE u.email IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY u.email, u.role ORDER BY total_events DESC LIMIT 20

content.description: Distinct active users per day
content.query:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', fe.created_at) AS day, COUNT(DISTINCT fe.user_id) AS active_users
FROM file_events fe GROUP BY day ORDER BY day

content.description: Total calls, tokens, and cost per LLM model
content.query:
SELECT model, COUNT(*) AS calls, SUM(total_tokens) AS total_tokens,
  ROUND(SUM(cost)::numeric, 4) AS total_cost_usd
FROM llm_call_events GROUP BY model ORDER BY calls DESC

content.description: Daily token usage per model
content.query:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('day', created_at) AS day, model, SUM(total_tokens) AS tokens
FROM llm_call_events GROUP BY day, model ORDER BY day

content.description: Weekly LLM cost, call count, and token totals
content.query:
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('week', created_at) AS week, ROUND(SUM(cost)::numeric, 4) AS total_cost_usd,
  COUNT(*) AS total_calls, SUM(total_tokens) AS total_tokens
FROM llm_call_events GROUP BY week ORDER BY week

documents.name: Avg LLM Call Duration by Model

content.description: Average duration and token count per LLM model
content.query:
SELECT model, COUNT(*) AS calls, ROUND(AVG(duration_s)::numeric, 2) AS avg_duration_s,
  ROUND(AVG(total_tokens)::numeric, 0) AS avg_tokens
FROM llm_call_events GROUP BY model ORDER BY calls DESC

content.description: File system activity analytics across modes and users
LLM API usage, cost, and performance stats
